content:
# Matthew Chapter 13 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_013_page_001.jpg, MAT_013_page_002.jpg, MAT_013_page_003.jpg

1. In that day, having gone out, the Yeshua from the house was sitting beside the sea.

2. And were gathered to him crowds great, so that he, into a boat having embarked, sat down; and all the crowd upon the shore stood.

3. And he spoke to them many things in parables, saying, "Behold, the sower went out to sow.

4. And in the sowing of him, some [seeds] fell beside the road, and the birds came and ate them.

5. And others fell upon the rocky [places], where they did not have much earth; and immediately they sprang up because of not having depth of earth.

6. And [the] sun having risen, they were scorched, and because of not having root, they withered.

7. And others fell upon the thorns, and the thorns came up and choked them.

8. And others fell upon the good earth and were giving fruit, some a hundred, and some sixty, and some thirty.

9. The [one] having ears, let him hear."

10. And having come, the disciples said to him, "Why in parables do you speak to them?"

11. And answering, he said to them, "Because to you it has been given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of the heavens, but to those it has not been given.

12. For whoever has, it will be given to him and he will have abundance; but whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.

13. Because of this in parables I speak to them, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear nor understand.

14. And is being fulfilled in them the prophecy of Esaias, the [one] saying, 'In hearing you will hear and not understand, and seeing you will see and not perceive.

15. For the heart of this people has become dull, and with the ears they hear heavily, and the eyes of them they closed, lest they see with the eyes and with the ears hear and with the heart understand and turn, and I will heal them.'

16. But blessed [are] the eyes of you because they see, and the ears of you because they hear.

17. For truly I say to you that many prophets and righteous [ones] desired to see what you see and did not see, and to hear what you hear and did not hear.

18. You therefore hear the parable of the sower.

19. Everyone hearing the word of the kingdom and not understanding, the evil [one] comes and snatches what has been sown in the heart of him. This is the [one] beside the road having been sown.

20. And the [one] upon the rocky [places] having been sown, this is the [one] hearing the word and immediately with joy receiving it;

21. but he has no root in himself, but is temporary; and [when] tribulation or persecution happens on account of the word, immediately he is scandalized.

22. And the [one] into the thorns having been sown, this is the [one] hearing the word, and the anxiety of the age and the deceitfulness of the riches choke the word, and unfruitful it becomes.

23. But the [one] upon the good earth having been sown, this is the [one] hearing the word and understanding, who indeed bears fruit and produces, some a hundred, some sixty, some thirty."

24. Another parable he set before them, saying, "The kingdom of the heavens has been likened to a man having sown good seed in the field of him.

25. But while the men slept, the enemy of him came and sowed weeds in the midst of the wheat, and went away.

26. And when the plant sprouted and made fruit, then appeared also the weeds.

27. And having come, the slaves of the householder said to him, 'Lord, did you not sow good seed in the field of you? From where then does it have weeds?'

28. And he said to them, 'An enemy man did this.' And the slaves say to him, 'Do you wish then, having gone, we gather them?'

29. And he says, 'No, lest gathering the weeds you uproot together with them the wheat.

30. Let both grow together until the harvest, and in the season of the harvest I will say to the reapers: Gather first the weeds and bind them into bundles to burn them, but the wheat gather into the storehouse of me.'"

31. Another parable he set before them, saying, "The kingdom of the heavens is like a grain of mustard, which a man having taken sowed in the field of him;

32. which indeed is smaller than all the seeds, but when it is grown, it is greater than the garden-plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the heaven come and nest in the branches of it."

33. Another parable he spoke to them: "The kingdom of the heavens is like leaven, which a woman having taken hid in three measures of flour until the whole was leavened."

34. All these things the Yeshua spoke in parables to the crowds, and without a parable he was not speaking to them,

35. so that what was spoken through the prophet might be fulfilled, saying, "I will open in parables the mouth of me; I will utter things having been hidden from [the] foundation of [the] world."

36. Then having dismissed the crowds, he came into the house. And came to him the disciples of him, saying, "Explain to us the parable of the weeds of the field."

37. And answering, he said, "The [one] sowing the good seed is the Son of the Man.

38. And the field is the world. And the good seed, these are the sons of the kingdom; and the weeds are the sons of the evil [one].

39. And the enemy, the [one] having sown them, is the devil. And the harvest is [the] completion of [the] age, and the reapers are angels.

40. Just as therefore the weeds are gathered and with fire burned, so it will be in the completion of the age.

41. The Son of the Man will send the angels of him, and they will gather out of the kingdom of him all the scandals and the [ones] doing the lawlessness,

42. and will cast them into the furnace of the fire; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.

43. Then the righteous will shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of the Father of them. The [one] having ears, let him hear.

44. The kingdom of the heavens is like a treasure having been hidden in the field, which a man having found hid, and from the joy of him goes and sells all, as much as he has, and buys the field that.

45. Again, the kingdom of the heavens is like a merchant man seeking beautiful pearls;

46. and having found one very-precious pearl, having gone, he has sold all, as much as he had, and bought it.

47. Again, the kingdom of the heavens is like a dragnet having been cast into the sea and having gathered of every kind;

48. which, when it was filled, having drawn up upon the shore and having sat down, they gathered the good into vessels but the rotten they cast out.

49. So it will be in the completion of the age: the angels will come out and will separate the evil from the midst of the righteous

50. and will cast them into the furnace of the fire; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.

51. Have you understood all these things?" They say to him, "Yes."

52. And he said to them, "Therefore every scribe having been discipled to the kingdom of the heavens is like a man, a householder, who brings out of the treasure of him new [things] and old."

53. And it came to pass [that] when the Yeshua finished these parables, he departed from there.

54. And having come into the fatherland of him, he was teaching them in the synagogue of them, so that they were astonished and said, "From where [does] this [man have] this wisdom and the mighty-works?

55. Is this not the son of the craftsman? Is not the mother of him called Mariam, and the brothers of him Iakobos and Ioses and Simon and Ioudas?

56. And the sisters of him, are they not all with us? From where then [does] this [man have] all these things?"

57. And they were scandalized in him. But the Yeshua said to them, "A prophet is not without honor except in the fatherland of him and in the house of him."

58. And he did not do there many mighty-works because of the unbelief of them.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **ζιζάνια (vv.25-40):** "Weeds" — specifically darnel (Lolium temulentum), a weed that closely resembles wheat during growth but is toxic when eaten. The parable depends on this botanical reality: you cannot distinguish darnel from wheat until harvest.

2. **κόσμος (v.38):** "World" — the Yeshua explicitly identifies the field as the κόσμος, not the church. This is significant for interpretation: the wheat and weeds coexist in the world, not within the community.

3. **v.35 — Psalm 78:2 citation:** The Sinaiticus attributes this to "the prophet" (τοῦ προφήτου) without naming the author. Some manuscripts add "Isaiah" erroneously. The quotation is from Psalm 78 (attributed to Asaph).

4. **Ἰωσῆς (v.55):** Sinaiticus reads "Ioses" (Ἰωσῆς), not "Joseph" (Ἰωσήφ). This is a different Greek form, possibly a diminutive or variant of the name.

5. **μαθητευθείς (v.52):** "Having been discipled" — a passive participle. The scribe has been made a disciple of the kingdom, not merely taught about it. This verb is unique to Matthew in the NT.

— EMET, AI Translator
